category_title: Underachieving students in 'difficult' contexts with ephemeral off-task behaviors
scenario_title: Ephemeral off-task behavior during independent workshop rotation
minimal_context: The student is practicing a gymnastics jump task using trampolines and mats in a small group workshop.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Look at the instruction sheet and practice the gymnastics jump using the trampoline and the mat.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Makes a correct jump and puts up the equipment) Okay, done. (Gives a kick to a mat and chats with friends in the group)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please get back in line and practice the jump again.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I work a bit but in the end it’s boring to do the same thing over and over again, so I have a laugh with my mates ...It’s no big deal, and after that I work as well.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just wait idly in line until it is your turn to go.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Doubles up while standing in line, then lies on the mats) This is taking too long. (Scans the environment and begins wandering about the gym in search of an opportunity for change)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Look at the instruction sheet and practice the gymnastics jump using the trampoline and the mat.

STUDENT: (Makes a correct jump and puts up the equipment) Okay, done. (Gives a kick to a mat and chats with friends in the group)

TUTOR: Please get back in line and practice the jump again.

STUDENT: I work a bit but in the end it’s boring to do the same thing over and over again, so I have a laugh with my mates ...It’s no big deal, and after that I work as well.

TUTOR: Just wait idly in line until it is your turn to go.

STUDENT: (Doubles up while standing in line, then lies on the mats) This is taking too long. (Scans the environment and begins wandering about the gym in search of an opportunity for change)

task.content: learn several motor skills at each workshop / gymnastics jump task

context.environment: gymnastics workshops were organized in parallel
context.materials: instruction sheets and equipment like mats and trampolines
context.social_setting: Students should work in groups of three to five people

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: experiencing boredom during passive moments (waiting in line)
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: made a correct jump, put up the equipment, chatted with friends, gave a kick to a mat, made another jump, wandered around, waited idly, did some off-task behavior, read the exercise sheet, and called to the teacher
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: scanned their environment looking for opportunities to escape boredom
related_quotes: I work a bit but in the end it’s boring to do the same thing over and over again, so I have a laugh with my mates ...It’s no big deal, and after that I work as well.

behavior_patterns.condition: when students doubled up while standing in line
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: lying on the mats, or began wandering about the gym in search of an opportunity for change
